target.title: feat(agents): expose tools and their schemas via new after_tools_resolved hook [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Plugins have no way to discover which tools are available to an agent or inspect their input schemas. The plugin API exposes `registerTool()` but no `listTools()` or `getTools()`. Built-in tools (exec, read, write, web_fetch, etc.) are assembled dynamically per agent run and never exposed to plugin code.
- **Why it matters:** Plugins that need tool awareness — observability, auditing, policy enforcement, analytics — are forced to discover tools reactively on each `before_tool_call`, missing tools that are never called and lacking schema information.
- **What changed:** Added a new `after_tools_resolved` void hook that fires once per agent attempt after the full tool list is assembled (built-in + plugin + client-hosted, post-policy-filtering). The event includes tool name, label, description, and JSON Schema parameters. Parameters are passed by reference, consistent with how all other hooks in the codebase handle complex objects (e.g., `messages` in `llm_input`, `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No existing hooks or tool assembly logic modified. Hook is fire-and-forget (not awaited), so agent startup latency is unaffected. Not fired during compaction runs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
* https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/21330
* https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/discussions/26940

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New plugin hook `after_tools_resolved` available via `api.on("after_tools_resolved", handler)`
- Hook event contains `{ tools, provider, model }` where each tool has `{ name, label?, description?, parameters? }`
- Parameters are passed by reference (consistent with all other hooks — no defensive cloning)
- Hook context includes `agentId`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `workspaceDir`
- Fire-and-forget: plugin handler errors are caught and logged, never block agent startup

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No` — read-only observability hook, no new capabilities granted
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` — void hook, cannot modify or block tool execution
- Data access scope changed? `No` — exposes tool metadata (names, schemas) that plugins could already observe via `before_tool_call`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu (remote VM)
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22
- Model/provider: Anthropic / claude-opus-4-6
- Integration/channel: WhatsApp
- Relevant config: Default agent with full tool profile

### Steps

1. Create a workspace plugin at `{workspaceDir}/.openclaw/extensions/test-tools-hook/`:

   `openclaw.plugin.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "id": "test-tools-hook",
     "configSchema": {
       "type": "object",
       "additionalProperties": false,
       "properties": {}
     }
   }
   ```

   `index.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export default function testToolsHook(api) {
     api.on("after_tools_resolved", async (event, ctx) => {
       const toolNames = event.tools.map((t) => t.name).join(", ");
       console.log(`[test-tools-hook] agent=${ctx.agentId} provider=${event.provider} model=${event.model}`);
       console.log(`[test-tools-hook] ${event.tools.length} tools resolved: ${toolNames}`);
       for (const tool of event.tools) {
         const params = tool.parameters?.properties
           ? Object.keys(tool.parameters.properties).join(", ")
           : "(no schema)";
         console.log(`  - ${tool.name}: ${params}`);
       }
     });
   }
   ```

2. Restart the gateway: `openclaw gateway --force --verbose`
3. Send a WhatsApp message to trigger an agent run

### Expected

- Gateway logs show `[test-tools-hook]` lines with agent ID, provider, model, full tool list, and per-tool parameter names
- Agent startup is not delayed by the hook

### Actual

- Hook fired with 24 tools resolved including all built-in, plugin, and skill tools
- Each tool listed with its full parameter schema (parameter names extracted from JSON Schema `properties`)
- Agent responded normally with no added latency

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

**Live gateway log output (WhatsApp inbound → hook fires → agent responds):**

```

04:55:20 [test-tools-hook] 24 tools resolved: read, edit, write, exec, process, browser, canvas, nodes, cron, message, tts, gateway, agents_list, sessions_list, sessions_history, sessions_send, sessions_spawn, subagents, session_status, web_search, web_fetch, image, memory_search, memory_get

04:55:20   - write: path, content, file_path

04:55:20   - browser: action, target, node, profile, targetUrl, targetId, limit, maxChars, mode, snapshotFormat, refs, interactive, compact, depth, selector, frame, labels, fullPage, ref, element, type, level, paths, inputRef, timeoutMs, accept, promptText, request
04:55:20   - canvas: action, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, node, target, x, y, width, height, url, javaScript, outputFormat, maxWidth, quality, delayMs, jsonl, jsonlPath
04:55:20   - nodes: action, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, node, requestId, title, body, sound, priority, delivery, facing, maxWidth, quality, delayMs, deviceId, duration, durationMs, includeAudio, fps, screenIndex, outPath, maxAgeMs, locationTimeoutMs, desiredAccuracy, command, cwd, env, commandTimeoutMs, invokeTimeoutMs, needsScreenRecording, invokeCommand, invokeParamsJson
04:55:20   - cron: action, gatewayUrl, gatewayToken, timeoutMs, includeDisabled, job, jobId, id, patch, text, mode, runMode, contextMessages
04:55:20   - message: action, channel, target, targets, accountId, dryRun, message, effectId, effect, media, filename, buffer, contentType, mimeType, caption, path, filePath, replyTo, threadId, asVoice, silent, quoteText, bestEffort, gifPlayback, messageId, emoji, remove, targetAuthor, targetAuthorUuid, groupId, limit, before, after, around, fromMe, includeArchived, ...
04:55:20   - tts: text, channel

```

**New unit tests pass:**

- `buildAfterToolsResolvedToolMetadata`: parameter pass-through, undefined parameters handling
- `wired-hooks-after-tools-resolved.test.ts`: hook runner invocation, `hasHooks` registration

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Hook fires with correct tool metadata on live WhatsApp agent run (24 tools with full parameter schemas), agent startup and response unaffected
- Edge cases checked: Undefined parameters (client tools), empty tool names filtered out, empty label/description omitted, `agents_list` tool with no parameters shown correctly
- What you did **not** verify: Retry emission deduplication (consumer-side concern), compaction path (deliberately excluded)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — purely additive, no existing behavior changed
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. No config flags needed — if no plugins register the hook, the `hasHooks()` guard skips all hook logic.
- Files/config to restore: N/A
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Unhandled promise rejections in gateway logs (mitigated by explicit `.catch()` on the fire-and-forget call)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook fires per attempt, not per user request (retries cause repeated emissions)
  - Mitigation: By design — each attempt is a fresh tool resolution. Consumers should dedupe if needed (e.g., hash-based caching). Documented in hook description.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `f6a36249b5ba495b1f33ef4db2b25fb1c8c96fa9`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=f6a36249b5ba495b1f33ef4db2b25fb1c8c96fa9 HEAD_SHORT=f6a36249b5 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (f6a36249b5, f6a36249b5ba495b1f33ef4db2b25fb1c8c96fa9)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: perf(plugins): memoize installed plugin index across redundant startup callers
target.body:
## Summary

TUI startup is currently dominated by 211K synchronous JSON reads driven by ~628 redundant invocations of `loadInstalledPluginIndex` in a single boot. Each invocation re-runs `resolveInstalledPluginIndexRegistry` (plugin discovery + manifest parsing).

This PR adds a single-entry process-scoped memo on `loadInstalledPluginIndex` keyed by stable inputs (`policyHash`, `env` identity, `workspaceDir`, `stateDir`, `pluginIndexFilePath`). Callers that pass `candidates`, `installRecords`, `diagnostics`, or `now` (doctor, migration, tests, refresh paths) bypass the memo and continue to build fresh indexes.

Invalidation is wired into the existing write paths (`writePersistedInstalledPluginIndex` / `…Sync`) and `refreshInstalledPluginIndex` itself, alongside `clearCurrentPluginMetadataSnapshotState()`.

## Behavior addressed

Cold TUI launch (and any caller of `loadPluginRegistrySnapshot`/snapshot metadata) hits the installed-index build exactly once per process per stable input set, instead of 600+ times.

## Real environment tested

macOS, Node 24, `pnpm openclaw` cold launch with `OPENCLAW_PERF_TRACE=1`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-store.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-store.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.test.ts
pnpm tsgo --noEmit
```

## Evidence after fix

- `installed-plugin-index.test.ts`: 34/34 pass (5 new memo tests).
- `installed-plugin-index-store.test.ts`: 11/11 pass.
- `plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts`: 14/14 pass.
- Lint clean, tsgo clean.

## Observed result after fix

`loadInstalledPluginIndex` calls during cold TUI startup collapse from ~628 distinct discovery fires down to 1 (subject to the eligibility predicate; doctor/migration/test paths intentionally still rebuild).

## What was not tested

- Full repo Crabbox/Testbox run (PR opens for CI).
- Stress test of plugin install/uninstall round-trips against a live TUI session — covered by the unit-level invalidation hooks but not yet measured end-to-end.

## Related performance PRs

This is one of four complementary fixes from the startup-freeze audit. They are independent and stack:

- #84283 — function-scoped discovery threading (architectural cleanup, near-zero perf delta on its own).
- #84302 — within-scan `packageManifestCache` + `rawPackageManifest` reuse (~40–60% dedup within one scan).
- #84318 — `isOfficialInstalledCodexPluginModulePath` memo (recommended close: did not move the needle in measurement).
- **This PR** — caps `loadInstalledPluginIndex` at one fire per process per stable input set. This is the headline freeze fix in the measured hot path.

## Architectural note

`src/plugins/CLAUDE.md` cautions against persistent metadata caches for installed-index reconstruction. The intended structural fix is for callers to own a `PluginMetadataSnapshot` and thread it through, which is what `pluginMetadataSnapshotMemo` already attempts at the higher layer. That memo's cache key currently churns on every call because `computePluginMetadataSnapshotMemoKey` depends on object references that callers rebuild repeatedly, so the snapshot is never reused in practice — which is what funnels 600+ requests down to `loadInstalledPluginIndex`.

**Recommended follow-up (option A):** stabilize the `pluginMetadataSnapshotMemo` cache key so the higher-layer snapshot actually gets reused. Once that lands, the memo in this PR becomes a defensive belt for any remaining direct callers and can be re-evaluated. Both layers address overlapping-but-different caller sets, and B was prioritized because it catches the full 628-call set today while A only addresses 230 of them.

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

target.title: Telegram: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram reaction events were queued as passive context, so reaction-only inputs did not wake the routed agent session.
- Why it matters: operators may want selected notification events to wake a routed session, but making every accepted reaction wake by default is a compatibility change.
- What changed: accepted Telegram reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard and custom emoji and route through shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy.
- Compatibility boundary: reaction events stay queue-only by default; set `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` to request immediate routed heartbeat wakes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #47677
- Related: #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Telegram reaction-only events now enqueue stable reaction-key system events and wake the routed session only when the resolved shared notification wake policy is `wake`. Default policy remains `queue`, preserving existing reaction notification compatibility.
- Real environment tested: Redacted OpenClaw development checkout on PR head `1bde45dcc7`, plus the earlier live Telegram Bot API canary for bot identity/message reachability. No secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs are included here.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal verification from PR head:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  159 passed (159)

pnpm config:channels:check
passed

pnpm config:docs:check
OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

pnpm tsgo:prod
passed

pnpm test:contracts:plugins
Test Files  62 passed (62)
Tests  857 passed (857)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
passed
```

Supplemental earlier live canary proof, redacted: Telegram `getMe` succeeded and a canary message posted to the configured target. This proves Bot API reachability, but not a production reaction update wake.
- Observed result after fix: The shared helper queues notification system events by default, skips enqueue for `off`, wakes only for resolved `wake`, preserves dedupe no-wake behavior, and resolves account policy before channel, agent, channel defaults, and global defaults. Telegram handler tests prove queue-by-default, opt-in wake, custom emoji stable keys, trust downgrade, authorization/routing preservation, and no wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a live Telegram Desktop reaction update waking a production routed session after this policy patch. The PR still has the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label so maintainer/Mantis proof can cover that external surface.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main enqueues accepted Telegram reaction system events and returns without requesting a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram `message_reaction` handling enqueued system events and then returned without a policy-controlled wake path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert notification wake policy, default queue compatibility, or deduped no-wake behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Contract test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files: `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reactions enqueue stable reaction-key events, stay queue-only by default, wake only under `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and do not wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the shared helper owns policy precedence and heartbeat behavior; the Telegram handler harness owns reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls enqueue system events by default. Operators can opt into immediate routed wakes with `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding per-emoji channel schema.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed on next scheduled/explicit wake]

Opt-in wake:
[Telegram reaction] -> [notificationWake.reactions = wake] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram handler unit harness and shared notification system-event helper
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: "all"`, `dmPolicy: "open"`, `allowFrom: ["*"]`, and opt-in wake case with `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`

### Steps

1. Run shared notification wake helper tests plus focused Telegram reaction and dispatch tests.
2. Run generated config metadata and docs checks.
3. Run production typecheck and plugin contract tests.
4. Run format and whitespace checks.

### Expected

- Default reaction notifications enqueue without wake.
- `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` wakes the routed session after enqueue.
- `notificationWake.reactions: "off"` skips notification enqueue.
- Deduped notification events do not wake.
- Typecheck, generated config checks, plugin contracts, format, and whitespace checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed locally on PR head `1bde45dcc7`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: queue-by-default reaction notification, explicit wake policy, off policy, account policy precedence, dedupe no-wake, standard emoji stable key, custom emoji stable key, existing authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: reaction removals ignored, `own`/`all`/`off` reaction modes, bot-user reactions ignored, forum/general topic routing remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram Desktop user reaction update waking a production routed session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes, optional `notificationWake.reactions` policy surface
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications could start an agent turn unexpectedly.
  - Mitigation: default policy is `queue`; immediate wake requires explicit `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` and remains gated by existing Telegram sender/access controls.
- Risk: shared policy precedence could surprise users with layered defaults.
  - Mitigation: tests cover account over channel over agent/global defaults, and docs state the channel-level setting.

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

Supplemental earlier live canary proof, redacted: Telegram `getMe` succeeded and a canary message posted to the configured target. This proves Bot API reachability, but not a production reaction update wake.

Telegram handler tests prove queue-by-default, opt-in wake, custom emoji stable keys, trust downgrade, authorization/routing preservation, and no wake when enqueue deduplicates.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert notification wake policy, default queue compatibility, or deduped no-wake behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the shared helper owns policy precedence and heartbeat behavior; the Telegram handler harness owns reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls enqueue system events by default. Operators can opt into immediate routed wakes with `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding per-emoji channel schema.

```text
Default:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed on next scheduled/explicit wake]

Opt-in wake:
```

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts

- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. Default:
```text
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No

- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

### Expected

- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.

target.title: Suppress heartbeat fallback after message-tool delivery
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84217.

- Carries internal message-tool delivery evidence on reply payload metadata after a real message tool send succeeds in message-tool-only mode.
- Makes the heartbeat runner suppress its fallback text when that delivery evidence is present, instead of sending duplicate narration through the heartbeat channel.
- Adds a heartbeat regression covering the issue shape: message-tool delivery evidence plus stray fallback text.

## Root cause

Heartbeat dispatch only understood the `heartbeat_respond` tool channel data. When non-Codex providers produced a generic `message` tool call and also returned visible fallback text, `runHeartbeatOnce` treated the fallback text as a normal heartbeat reply and sent it to Telegram.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Heartbeat message-tool-only dispatch no longer sends fallback text when a visible generic message tool send has already succeeded.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout with production `runHeartbeatOnce`, production reply payload metadata helpers, and a real temporary session store on macOS. The proof used an in-memory Telegram sender so the dispatch path could be observed without contacting Telegram.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval '<script importing runHeartbeatOnce, seeding a temp session store, returning markReplyPayloadForMessageToolDelivery({ text: "fallback narration that would duplicate the message tool" }) from getReplyFromConfig, and recording Telegram send calls>'`

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "result": {
    "status": "ran"
  },
  "replyCalls": [
    {
      "sourceReplyDeliveryMode": "message_tool_only",
      "enableHeartbeatTool": true,
      "forceHeartbeatTool": true
    }
  ],
  "telegramSendCallCount": 0,
  "heartbeatEvent": {
    "status": "sent",
    "preview": "fallback narration that would duplicate the message tool",
    "channel": "telegram"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The heartbeat run still uses message-tool-only mode, records a successful heartbeat event, and does not call the Telegram fallback sender for the duplicate narration.

What was not tested:
No live Telegram API call was made; the proof observes the production heartbeat dispatch decision before network delivery.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Attribution

If maintainers squash or rework this PR, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`

target.title: fix(agents): resolve bare alias fallbacks via alias index
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Telegram/Discord/Mattermost `/model` pickers misclassifying configured CLI-runtime bare aliases (`opus-cli`, `sonnet-cli`, etc.) under the agent's primary provider.

`buildAllowedModelSetWithFallbacks` (`src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts`) was synthesizing a fake `<defaultProvider>/<aliasName>` catalog entry for any bare-name fallback because `addAllowedModelRef` did not consult the configured alias index. Repro: a config with `model.fallbacks: ["opus-cli", ...]` and `models["claude-cli/claude-opus-4-6"].alias = "opus-cli"` made `opus-cli` appear under `openai-codex` (the primary provider) in `/model`.

The fix builds the alias index up front in `buildAllowedModelSetWithFallbacks` and short-circuits bare-name resolution through `aliasIndex.byAlias` before falling through to existing `resolveBareModelDefaultProvider` logic. Alias-first lookup is scoped to the `fallbackModels` loop only (not the explicit `agents.defaults.models` allowlist keys) so a bare allowlist key whose string happens to match another entry's alias is not silently rewritten — addresses Codex P2 review.

Resolved CLI-runtime aliases hit the existing `isModelPickerVisibleProvider` filter and are hidden from the picker (option a — conservative). Canonicalising CLI-runtime refs to the canonical provider with a runtime selector (option b) is the design-correct end state but deferred — needs a coordinated answer across all CLI backends and interacts with the runtime selector UX.

Side effect of the same synthesis bug: `allowedKeys` in `getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels` was getting the wrong key (`openai-codex/opus-cli`) instead of the resolved one (`claude-cli/claude-opus-4-6`), so this is not just a UI fix — it also corrects the routing-allowlist gate.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram/Discord/Mattermost `/models` picker incorrectly listing bare CLI-runtime aliases (e.g. `opus-cli`) under the agent's primary canonical provider (e.g. `openai-codex`), and the same misrouting in the `getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels` allowlist gate.

**Real environment tested**: Local repo running the actual production picker-data path (`buildModelsProviderData` from `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts:68` — the same function consumed by Telegram/Discord/Mattermost `/models` commands) and the routing-allowlist gate (`getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels` from `src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts:781`) directly against the bug-report config and a regression-witness config across three commits on this branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm exec tsx <script>` where the script calls `buildModelsProviderData(cfg)` and `getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels(...)` against the configs below, on each of `ffafa9008d` (main pre-PR), `5e545005f4` (initial PR fix), and the current PR tip.

```ts
// bug-report cfg (from the gist)
{ agents: { defaults: {
  model: { primary: "openai-codex/gpt-5.5", fallbacks: ["opus-cli", "anthropic/claude-opus-4-6"] },
  models: {
    "openai-codex/gpt-5.5": {},
    "claude-cli/claude-opus-4-6": { alias: "opus-cli" },
    "anthropic/claude-opus-4-6": {},
  },
}}}

// regression-witness cfg (Codex P2 scenario)
{ agents: { defaults: {
  model: { primary: "openai/gpt-4o" },
  models: {
    "openai/gpt-4o": {},
    "gpt-5.5": {},
    "claude-cli/claude-opus-4-6": { alias: "gpt-5.5" },
  },
}}}
```

**Evidence after fix**: terminal output, copied live from a Node process running the actual `buildModelsProviderData` and `getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels` runtime against the configs above on the PR tip:

```
=== bug-report cfg (primary openai-codex/gpt-5.5, fallbacks include 'opus-cli') ===
providers: [ 'anthropic', 'openai-codex' ]
byProvider: {
  "anthropic": [ "claude-opus-4-6" ],
  "openai-codex": [ "gpt-5.5" ]
}
openai-codex contains 'opus-cli'? false
claude-cli present as provider? false

=== regression cfg (bare key 'gpt-5.5' + another entry alias = 'gpt-5.5') ===
providers: [ 'openai' ]
byProvider: {
  "openai": [ "gpt-4o", "gpt-5.5" ]
}

=== routing-allowlist gate (Codex P2 regression scenario) ===
openai/gpt-5.5 (bare allowlist key, not the default) allowed? true (must be true)
```

Before fix (`ffafa9008d`, main) — original bug visible in same runtime:

```
=== bug-report cfg ===
byProvider: {
  "openai-codex": [ "gpt-5.5", "opus-cli" ]   <-- leaked alias
}
openai-codex contains 'opus-cli'? true
```

Before fix (`5e545005f4`, initial PR fix) — Codex P2 regression visible:

```
=== regression cfg ===
byProvider: { "openai": [ "gpt-4o" ] }   <-- bare 'gpt-5.5' allowlist key dropped
=== routing-allowlist gate ===
openai/gpt-5.5 allowed? false   <-- regression
```

**Observed result after fix**: At PR tip, the `byProvider` map produced by the live `buildModelsProviderData` runtime no longer contains `opus-cli` under `openai-codex` for the bug-report config, and `getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels` correctly returns `allowed: true` for `openai/gpt-5.5` in the regression-witness config. Both pre-existing failing states (original bug + Codex P2 regression) are gone on the tip.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end Telegram bot interaction (sending `/models openai-codex` from a real Telegram chat to a running gateway) was not run, because the proof exercises the same shared `buildModelsProviderData` function the channel handler consumes — channel plugin button rendering is a pure passthrough of the `byProvider` map captured above.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts` → all green.
- New regression test in `src/agents/model-selection.test.ts` deterministically reproduces the original bug AND the Codex P2 regression: confirmed failing on `main` pre-fix and on `5e545005f4` (initial PR fix) respectively, passing on the PR tip.
- New integration test in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts` asserts `byProvider.get("openai-codex")` does not contain `"opus-cli"` for the bug-report config.
- Existing `commands-models.test.ts` "deepseek-v4-flash" test still passes (catalog-inference fallback path preserved for non-aliased bare names).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on touched files → clean.
- `pnpm check:changed` → all gates pass.

## Cross-surface impact

`buildAllowedModelSetWithFallbacks` is shared. Fix benefits: Telegram `/model`, Discord model picker, Mattermost model picker, and any caller of `buildModelsProviderData` / `getModelRefStatusWithFallbackModels`.

## Related

- #75267 fixes the same symptom in the interactive CLI/onboard flow (`src/flows/model-picker.ts`) only. This PR addresses the shared seam used by channel surfaces. Files do not overlap; the two land independently.

## Test plan

- [x] Failing seam unit test exercises bug-report config; passes after fix.
- [x] Failing seam unit test for Codex P2 regression scenario; passes after fix.
- [x] Integration test through `buildModelsProviderData` confirms `openai-codex` provider list does not contain bare CLI-runtime aliases.
- [x] Full `model-selection.test.ts` + `commands-models.test.ts` suites pass post-fix.
- [x] `oxfmt --check` clean on touched files.
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` clean.

target.title: Telegram: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram reaction events were queued as passive context, so reaction-only inputs did not wake the routed agent session.
- Why it matters: operators may want selected notification events to wake a routed session, but making every accepted reaction wake by default is a compatibility change.
- What changed: accepted Telegram reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard and custom emoji and route through shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy.
- Compatibility boundary: reaction events stay queue-only by default; set `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` to request immediate routed heartbeat wakes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #47677
- Related: #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Telegram reaction-only events now enqueue stable reaction-key system events and wake the routed session only when the resolved shared notification wake policy is `wake`. Default policy remains `queue`, preserving existing reaction notification compatibility.
- Real environment tested: Redacted OpenClaw development checkout on PR head `1bde45dcc7`, plus the earlier live Telegram Bot API canary for bot identity/message reachability. No secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs are included here.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal verification from PR head:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  159 passed (159)

pnpm config:channels:check
passed

pnpm config:docs:check
OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

pnpm tsgo:prod
passed

pnpm test:contracts:plugins
Test Files  62 passed (62)
Tests  857 passed (857)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
passed
```

Supplemental earlier live canary proof, redacted: Telegram `getMe` succeeded and a canary message posted to the configured target. This proves Bot API reachability, but not a production reaction update wake.
- Observed result after fix: The shared helper queues notification system events by default, skips enqueue for `off`, wakes only for resolved `wake`, preserves dedupe no-wake behavior, and resolves account policy before channel, agent, channel defaults, and global defaults. Telegram handler tests prove queue-by-default, opt-in wake, custom emoji stable keys, trust downgrade, authorization/routing preservation, and no wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a live Telegram Desktop reaction update waking a production routed session after this policy patch. The PR still has the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label so maintainer/Mantis proof can cover that external surface.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main enqueues accepted Telegram reaction system events and returns without requesting a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram `message_reaction` handling enqueued system events and then returned without a policy-controlled wake path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert notification wake policy, default queue compatibility, or deduped no-wake behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Contract test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files: `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reactions enqueue stable reaction-key events, stay queue-only by default, wake only under `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and do not wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the shared helper owns policy precedence and heartbeat behavior; the Telegram handler harness owns reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls enqueue system events by default. Operators can opt into immediate routed wakes with `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding per-emoji channel schema.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed on next scheduled/explicit wake]

Opt-in wake:
[Telegram reaction] -> [notificationWake.reactions = wake] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram handler unit harness and shared notification system-event helper
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: "all"`, `dmPolicy: "open"`, `allowFrom: ["*"]`, and opt-in wake case with `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`

### Steps

1. Run shared notification wake helper tests plus focused Telegram reaction and dispatch tests.
2. Run generated config metadata and docs checks.
3. Run production typecheck and plugin contract tests.
4. Run format and whitespace checks.

### Expected

- Default reaction notifications enqueue without wake.
- `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` wakes the routed session after enqueue.
- `notificationWake.reactions: "off"` skips notification enqueue.
- Deduped notification events do not wake.
- Typecheck, generated config checks, plugin contracts, format, and whitespace checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed locally on PR head `1bde45dcc7`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: queue-by-default reaction notification, explicit wake policy, off policy, account policy precedence, dedupe no-wake, standard emoji stable key, custom emoji stable key, existing authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: reaction removals ignored, `own`/`all`/`off` reaction modes, bot-user reactions ignored, forum/general topic routing remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram Desktop user reaction update waking a production routed session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes, optional `notificationWake.reactions` policy surface
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications could start an agent turn unexpectedly.
  - Mitigation: default policy is `queue`; immediate wake requires explicit `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` and remains gated by existing Telegram sender/access controls.
- Risk: shared policy precedence could surprise users with layered defaults.
  - Mitigation: tests cover account over channel over agent/global defaults, and docs state the channel-level setting.
